mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate revenue contraction, causing the S&P 500 to decline over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and industrial production indicate a synchronized demand shock across consumer and business sectors, which compresses equity risk premiums and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not systematically fall below zero when both proxies decline concurrently, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle where the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0 percent, rising consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces valuation multiple compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during periods where both the funds rate and CPI index are simultaneously elevated, or the predictive slope coefficient flips sign in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for publicly traded companies, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP acceleration consistently fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns, or the rolling correlation between GDP growth surprises and SPX returns drops below 0.10 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in residential construction permits below 1.5 million annualized units signals a collapse in household wealth and consumer confidence, triggering a sustained decline in S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when housing starts drop below the 1500 threshold, or the signal generates a negative risk-adjusted return over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and stagnant industrial output indicates a structural shift toward services that suppresses corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-quarter horizon.
failure_condition: The spread between employment and industrial growth fails to predict negative equity returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance when controlling for monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of sustained industrial production growth above 102, supply chain bottlenecks resolve and input cost deflation occurs, which expands corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns decline or remain flat when industrial production exceeds the 102 threshold, or the forward return distribution shows no positive skew during high-output regimes.
